page: Amazon Web Services i3en.3xlarge is a storage-optimized instance built on the Nitro hypervisor. It features an Intel Xeon 8259CL processor with an x86_64 architecture, providing 12 vCPUs across 6 physical cores with hyper-threading enabled. The CPU operates at a clock speed of 3.1 GHz and includes a 35 MB L3 cache. Memory configuration consists of 96.0 GB of DDR4 RAM operating at 3 GHz, yielding 8.0 GB of memory per physical core.

A primary feature of this instance is its bundled local storage, consisting of 7500 GB of NVMe SSD capacity. It does not include any GPU hardware. Networking capabilities include a baseline bandwidth of 12 Gbps.

Benchmark data indicates a clear performance tradeoff. Single-core performance is weak, falling into the bottom 25% in stress-ng and Geekbench single-core tests, as well as single-threaded gzip compression. However, multi-core performance scales to average levels, as demonstrated by stress-ng multi-core, Geekbench multi-core, and bzip3 multi-threaded decompression tests. Memory bandwidth, Passmark CPU scores, and Redis SET operations also perform in the middle 50% tier. Large-model LLM inference is a bottleneck, with prompt processing for 7B models rated as poor.

From a cost-efficiency perspective, the instance offers high local storage density and memory capacity relative to its compute resources, making it a viable option for storage-heavy workloads without the premium of high-tier compute-optimized instances. It is suitable for database hosting, distributed file systems, data warehousing, and applications requiring high-capacity, low-latency local NVMe storage.
description: Amazon Web Services i3en.3xlarge is a storage-optimized instance featuring an Intel Xeon 8259CL processor with 12 vCPUs, 6 physical cores, and 96.0 GB of DDR4 memory. Built on the Nitro hypervisor, it includes 7500 GB of local NVMe SSD storage and a baseline network bandwidth of 12 Gbps, with no GPU accelerators. Performance benchmarks show a distinct tradeoff: single-core CPU execution and single-threaded compression are weak, falling into the bottom 25%, while multi-core processing, memory bandwidth, and Redis SET operations achieve average tier results. The instance's high storage-to-compute ratio provides qualitative cost efficiency for data-dense environments. It is designed for workloads such as database hosting, transactional systems, and distributed file storage that require substantial local NVMe capacity and moderate multi-core processing power.
og_description: Amazon Web Services i3en.3xlarge is a storage-optimized instance featuring 12 vCPUs, 96 GB DDR4 RAM, and 7500 GB of local NVMe SSD storage, delivering average multi-core and weak single-core performance.
meta_description: Amazon Web Services i3en.3xlarge features 12 vCPUs, 96 GB RAM, and 7500 GB of local NVMe SSD storage, optimized for database and storage workloads.
tagline: A storage-optimized instance combining 7.5 TB of local NVMe SSD capacity with balanced multi-core performance for data-intensive workloads.
bullet_points: Intel Xeon 8259CL processor with 12 vCPUs and 96 GB DDR4 RAM
7500 GB of high-capacity local NVMe SSD storage
12 Gbps baseline network bandwidth on the Nitro hypervisor
Average multi-core performance contrasted with weak single-core benchmarks
Suited for database hosting, transactional systems, and distributed storage
